Name: Documentation refresh
Description: This description is intentionally long so the table renderer has to wrap it across multiple lines while keeping the row readable and aligned with the task hierarchy.

Name: README rewrite
Description: Normal prose wraps at word boundaries. The row grows taller so the full sentence stays visible inside the Description column.

Name: CLI examples
Description: Single long tokens are not split automatically, so this intentionally ridiculous identifier shows the edge case: SuperLongUnbrokenTokenThatWillNotWordWrapEvenThoughEverythingElseDoes

Name: Docs sign-off
Description: Milestone descriptions wrap the same way as task descriptions in table mode.

Name: Release preparation
Description: A second phase makes the table more realistic and shows that wrapped rows can coexist with short rows without breaking the grid.

Name: Announcement draft
Description: Another longer paragraph that wraps naturally and demonstrates the intended behavior for human-written descriptions.
